 {"id":"rich_rust-26r","title":"Verify Table Width Expand Algorithm","description":"## Objective
Verify expand_widths() in Table matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9 exactly.

## Background
When table has extra space available, expand_widths() distributes it among columns based on their ratios. This uses the ratio distribution algorithm.

## Technical Details

### Spec Requirement (Section 9.4)
expand_widths() should:
1. Calculate excess = available_width - current_total
2. Distribute extra space using ratio_distribute()
3. Columns without explicit ratio get default ratio of 1
4. Columns with ratio=0 get no extra space

### Algorithm Verification
```
Given: 3 columns with widths [20, 20, 20] = 60
       ratios [1, 2, 1] (second column gets 2x share)
       available width = 100
       excess = 40

Step 1: Calculate ratio distribution
  total_ratio = 1 + 2 + 1 = 4
  col0 gets: 40 * (1/4) = 10
  col1 gets: 40 * (2/4) = 20
  col2 gets: 40 * (1/4) = 10

Result: [30, 40, 30]
```

### Test Cases
1. Equal ratios - equal distribution
2. Different ratios - proportional distribution
3. Zero ratio column - gets no extra space
4. Single column - gets all extra
5. Rounding edge cases

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (expand_widths function)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4, Section 14","acceptance_criteria":"expand_widths uses ratio_distribute correctly. All test cases pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:10.385823223-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:36:54.908252436-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:36:54.908252436-05:00","close_reason":"Validated: expand_widths() matches RICH_SPEC Section 14.4 exactly. Tests pass:
- test_expand_widths_with_ratios: widths [20,20,20] + ratios [1,2,1] = [30,40,30] ✓
- test_expand_widths_minimal_ratio_case: ratio 1:3 distribution works ✓
Implementation uses Ratio<usize> for exact fractional arithmetic. See table.rs:700-747.","labels":["critical","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-26r","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.835304272-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-26r","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q5f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:58.139024109-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-2ah","title":"Verify API Ergonomics and Rust Idioms","description":"Review and improve API ergonomics to ensure idiomatic Rust usage.

PURPOSE:
Ensure rich_rust provides a pleasant, idiomatic Rust API that follows community conventions and leverages Rust type system effectively.

ERGONOMICS CHECKLIST:

1. BUILDER PATTERNS
- [ ] All complex structs use builder pattern
- [ ] Builders are chainable (return Self)
- [ ] Builders have sensible defaults
- [ ] Terminal build() method returns owned type
- [ ] Example: Table::new().title("X").add_row(...).build()

2. FROM IMPLEMENTATIONS
- [ ] From<&str> for Text
- [ ] From<String> for Text
- [ ] From<&str> for Color (if infallible)
- [ ] TryFrom<&str> for Color (if fallible)
- [ ] From<(u8, u8, u8)> for ColorTriplet
- [ ] Into<Style> for common types

3. DEFAULT IMPLEMENTATIONS
- [ ] All public structs with sensible defaults impl Default
- [ ] Default creates useful zero-config instances
- [ ] Example: Console::default() works reasonably

4. DISPLAY IMPLEMENTATIONS
- [ ] Debug for all public types
- [ ] Display where semantic (Color, Style descriptions)
- [ ] Error types impl std::error::Error

5. CLONE/COPY SEMANTICS
- [ ] Small value types are Copy (ColorTriplet)
- [ ] Larger types are Clone
- [ ] Consider Cow<str> for string-heavy types

6. GENERIC PARAMETERS
- [ ] Functions accept impl AsRef<str> where appropriate
- [ ] Use Into<T> for owned conversions
- [ ] Avoid overly generic signatures that hurt error messages

7. ERROR HANDLING
- [ ] Custom error types with helpful messages
- [ ] Errors implement std::error::Error
- [ ] Consider anyhow/thiserror patterns

8. ITERATORS
- [ ] Types that contain collections expose iterators
- [ ] IntoIterator implementations
- [ ] Iterator adapters where useful

FILES TO REVIEW:
- All public API surfaces in src/*.rs
- src/prelude.rs exports

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- API feels natural to Rust developers
- No clippy warnings on API usage
- Documentation examples compile cleanly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:25.444405212-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:57:54.100141652-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:57:54.100141652-05:00","close_reason":"VERIFIED: API follows Rust idioms excellently. Findings: (1) Builder patterns extensively used (Style, Console, Table, Column, Panel, Columns, Align, PrintOptions) with #[must_use] annotations. (2) From/TryFrom implementations comprehensive (Color, ColorTriplet, Style, Text, PaddingDimensions). (3) Default implemented on all public structs. (4) Display/Debug on all appropriate types. (5) Clone/Copy correctly categorized. (6) Error types implement std::error::Error. (7) 133+ methods use builder pattern. The API is well-designed and idiomatic."}
 {"id":"rich_rust-2oy","title":"EPIC: Documentation & Examples","description":"## Objective
Create comprehensive documentation and examples for rich_rust users.

## Background
Good documentation is essential for adoption. This includes:
1. API documentation (rustdoc)
2. User guide with examples
3. Migration guide from Python Rich
4. Example programs

## Scope

### API Documentation
- Module-level documentation
- Struct/function documentation
- Code examples in doc comments

### User Guide
- Getting started
- Core concepts (Console, Style, Text, Segment)
- Renderables (Table, Panel, Tree, etc.)
- Advanced topics

### Examples
- Basic usage
- Tables
- Panels
- Progress bars
- Custom renderables

### Migration Guide
- Python Rich -> rich_rust mapping
- Feature parity table
- Known differences

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs documented
- Examples compile and run
- User can learn from docs without source code","acceptance_criteria":"All public APIs documented. Examples work.  {"id":"rich_rust-3oo","title":"Console missing render_lines() helper method","description":"## Issue

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 7.6, Console should have a `render_lines()` helper method that renders content to list of lines, each line being a list of segments.

This method is useful for:
- Table cell rendering
- Panel/box content wrapping
- Layouts that need line-by-line access

## Signature from Spec
```rust
fn render_lines(
    &self,
    renderable: &dyn Renderable,
    options: &ConsoleOptions,
    style: Option<&Style>,
    pad: bool,
    new_lines: bool,
) -> Vec<Vec<Segment>>
```

## Fix
Implement render_lines() method in Console struct following the spec.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T23:45:52.166325908-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:01:42.835981625-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:01:42.835981625-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented. render_lines() and render_lines_with_options() methods exist at src/console.rs:578-653 with comprehensive tests at lines 849-953. Methods handle line splitting, padding, width constraints, and newline appending.","labels":["console","implementation"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-4ot","title":"Set Up Golden File Test Infrastructure","description":"Create golden file (snapshot) testing infrastructure for visual regression detection.

PURPOSE:
Golden file tests capture expected rendering output and detect visual regressions by comparing actual output against stored expectations. This is CRITICAL for a terminal rendering library.

INFRASTRUCTURE REQUIREMENTS:

1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE:
   tests/golden/
   - fixtures/           (Input data for tests)
   - expected/           (Golden output files)
   - golden_test.rs      (Test runner)

2. TEST RUNNER FEATURES:
   - Compare actual vs expected output
   - Strip ANSI codes option for text-only comparison
   - Generate diff on failure
   - Auto-update mode (GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 env var)
   - Detailed failure messages with line-by-line diff

3. REQUIRED GOLDEN FILES (minimum):
   - table_basic.golden
   - table_styled.golden
   - panel_simple.golden
   - panel_nested.golden
   - text_wrapped.golden
   - rule_variants.golden
   - tree_nested.golden
   - columns_layout.golden

4. IMPLEMENTATION OPTIONS:
   - Use insta crate for snapshot testing
   - OR custom implementation with file comparison

5. LOGGING:
   - Use tracing on comparison functions
   - Log: test name, expected path, lengths
   - On failure: log diff location

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Golden file infrastructure compiles and runs
- At least 8 golden files created
- Auto-update mode works
- Failures produce readable diffs
- CI integration documented","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:43.197631197-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:14:04.764355724-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:14:04.764355724-05:00","close_reason":"Golden file test infrastructure complete: created tests/golden_test.rs with 31 snapshot tests covering Rule (4), Panel (6), Table (7), Tree (3), Columns (2), ProgressBar (4), and combined/edge-case tests (5). Generated 31 snapshot files in tests/snapshots/. All 37 tests pass.","labels":["critical","infrastructure","testing"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-yz6","title":"EPIC: Performance Optimization","description":"## Objective
Optimize rich_rust for production performance, focusing on hot paths identified through profiling.

## Background
Terminal rendering can be performance-critical in CLI tools. Key optimizations include:
1. Caching frequently computed values
2. Minimizing allocations in hot paths
3. Efficient string building for ANSI output
4. Lazy evaluation where possible

## Scope

### Caching (High Priority)
- LRU cache for color parsing
- Cell width caching for repeated strings
- Style ANSI code caching

### Allocation Reduction
- Reuse buffers in rendering loop
- SmallVec for small collections
- String interning for common values

### Algorithm Optimization
- Table width calculation
- Text wrapping
- Segment merging

### Benchmarks
- Establish baseline benchmarks
- Measure improvement after each optimization
- Prevent regressions

## Success Criteria
- Measurable improvement in benchmark suite
- No performance regressions
- Acceptable memory footprint","acceptance_criteria":"Benchmarks show improvement. No regressions.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:26.604866419-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:54:34.276757594-05:00"}
 {"id":"rich_rust-zbw","title":"Create Regression Test Suite","description":"## Objective
Create dedicated regression test suite to catch behavioral changes.

## Background
Regressions are bugs that reintroduce previously fixed issues or change expected behavior. A regression suite specifically tests known past issues and edge cases.

## Implementation

### Regression Test Structure
```
tests/
  regression/
    mod.rs           # Regression test module
    issue_001.rs     # Named after issue/bug
    issue_002.rs
    ...
```

### Regression Test Format
Each regression test must document:
1. What bug it prevents
2. When it was introduced
3. What the fix was
4. How to reproduce

```rust
/// Regression test for issue #42
/// 
/// Bug: Table width calculation overflowed when sum of minimums exceeded usize::MAX / 2
/// Fixed: 2024-01-15 in commit abc123
/// 
/// This test ensures the fix remains in place.
#[test]
fn regression_issue_42_table_width_overflow() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    info!("Regression test: issue #42 - table width overflow");
    
    // Reproduce the conditions that caused the bug
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("A").min_width(usize::MAX / 4))
        .add_column(Column::new("B").min_width(usize::MAX / 4))
        .add_column(Column::new("C").min_width(usize::MAX / 4));
    
    // This should not panic or overflow
    let result = std::panic::catch_unwind(|| {
        table.calculate_widths(100);
    });
    
    assert!(result.is_ok(), "Table width calculation panicked");
    info!("Regression test PASSED");
}
```

### Categories
1. **Parsing regressions**: Color, style, markup parsing edge cases
2. **Layout regressions**: Table width, text wrap, alignment
3. **Rendering regressions**: ANSI output, Unicode handling
4. **Performance regressions**: Speed, memory usage

### Running Regression Tests
```bash
# Run all regression tests
cargo test regression --features all

# Run specific category
cargo test regression::parsing
```

## Files to Create
- tests/regression/mod.rs
- tests/regression/*.rs (one per regression)","acceptance_criteria":"Regression suite established. Each test documents the bug it prevents.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:46.701299753-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:51:36.50075742-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:51:36.50075742-05:00","close_reason":"Created tests/regression_tests.rs with 35 comprehensive regression tests covering parsing, layout, and rendering categories. File includes documentation, test logging, and phased test organization as specified.","labels":["regression","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zbw","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.344645233-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zbw","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.149145789-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-zca","title":"Add Unit Tests for Text Module","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/text.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Text Construction
- [ ] Text::new()
- [ ] Text::plain(text)
- [ ] Text::styled(text, style)
- [ ] From<String> and From<&str>

### Text Manipulation
- [ ] append() adds plain text
- [ ] append_styled() adds styled text
- [ ] stylize() applies span
- [ ] Overlapping spans
- [ ] Adjacent spans

### Text Rendering
- [ ] render() produces segments
- [ ] Styles applied correctly
- [ ] Tab expansion
- [ ] Base style + spans

### Text Wrapping
- [ ] wrap() at word boundaries
- [ ] wrap() breaks long words
- [ ] wrap() respects overflow mode
- [ ] wrap() with wide characters
- [ ] no_wrap flag

### Text Division (CRITICAL)
- [ ] divide() basic split
- [ ] divide() with spans crossing boundaries
- [ ] divide() span starts at cut
- [ ] divide() span ends at cut
- [ ] divide() multiple spans
- [ ] divide() empty divisions

### Justification
- [ ] Left justify
- [ ] Right justify
- [ ] Center justify
- [ ] Full justify

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/text.rs (expand tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for text.rs. divide() thoroughly tested.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Text module tests are CRITICAL and must have extensive logging:

1. **Text construction**: Log plain text and initial spans
2. **Stylize operations**: Log start/end positions and style
3. **Wrap operations**: Log width, input length, output line count
4. **Divide operations**: Log offsets and resulting parts WITH spans

Example for divide() - the most critical test:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_text_divide_with_spans() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("test_divide_spans");
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let mut text = Text::plain("hello world!");
    info!(plain = %text.plain, "Initial text");
    
    text.stylize(6, 11, Style::new().bold());
    debug!(spans = ?text.spans, "After stylize");
    
    let parts = text.divide(&[5, 10]);
    for (i, part) in parts.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            index = i,
            plain = %part.plain,
            spans = ?part.spans,
            "Divided part"
        );
    }
    
    // Detailed assertions with logging
    assert_eq!(parts[0].plain, "hello", "Part 0 content");
    assert!(parts[0].spans.is_empty(), "Part 0 should have no spans");
    debug!(part = 0, "Verified: no spans");
    
    assert_eq!(parts[1].plain, " worl", "Part 1 content");
    assert!(!parts[1].spans.is_empty(), "Part 1 should have span");
    debug!(part = 1, span = ?parts[1].spans[0], "Verified: has span");
    
    info!("Text divide with spans PASSED");
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:47.51982787-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:11:47.199758675-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:11:47.199758675-05:00","close_reason":"Added 55 unit tests to Text module (19→74 total). Coverage: construction (new, styled, From), overlapping/adjacent/nested spans, divide() with span boundary cases (CRITICAL), wrap at word boundaries, overflow modes, justification (left/right/center/full), slice edge cases, tab expansion, span helpers. All 74 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zca","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.783337024-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

Add property-based tests using proptest or quickcheck for invariant verification.

## Test Properties

### Color Properties
- [ ] parse(x).to_hex() roundtrip preserves value (for valid hex)
- [ ] downgrade never increases color resolution
- [ ] all standard colors have valid ANSI codes

### Style Properties
- [ ] combine is associative: (a+b)+c = a+(b+c)
- [ ] null is identity: null+s = s, s+null = s
- [ ] parse(render(s)) roundtrip (where applicable)

### Text Properties
- [ ] wrap(w).join() preserves content (modulo whitespace)
- [ ] divide(cuts).concat() = original
- [ ] cell_length(wrap(w)) <= w for all lines

### Segment Properties
- [ ] split_at_cell(n) left + right = original
- [ ] simplify preserves rendered content
- [ ] divide(cuts) total length = original length

### Measurement Properties
- [ ] minimum <= maximum always
- [ ] clamp(a,b).minimum >= a
- [ ] clamp(a,b).maximum <= b
- [ ] union is commutative and associative

## Dependencies
- proptest crate in dev-dependencies

## Files to Create/Modify
- Cargo.toml (add proptest)
- tests/property_tests.rs (new file)","acceptance_criteria":"Property tests pass with 1000+ cases each.  {"id":"rich_rust-zxq","title":"Create Example Programs","description":"## Objective
Create comprehensive example programs demonstrating rich_rust features.

## Examples to Create

### examples/basic.rs
- Console creation
- Print styled text
- Basic markup usage

### examples/tables.rs
- Simple table
- Table with headers
- Styled cells
- Column alignment
- Different box styles

### examples/panels.rs
- Basic panel
- Panel with title/subtitle
- Nested panels
- Different border styles

### examples/text_styling.rs
- Colors (named, hex, RGB)
- Attributes (bold, italic, underline)
- Combined styles
- Hyperlinks

### examples/layout.rs
- Alignment
- Columns
- Padding
- Rules (horizontal lines)

### examples/progress.rs (if implemented)
- Progress bar
- Spinner
- Multiple progress bars

### examples/tree.rs
- Basic tree
- Styled tree
- Custom guides

### examples/kitchen_sink.rs
- Comprehensive demo of all features
- Good for visual testing

## Implementation
Each example should:
- Be self-contained
- Have clear comments
- Demonstrate one feature area
